untranslatable_reason: Head-and-shoulders pattern detection requires multi-point geometric pattern recognition (identifying shoulders A, C, E and neckline B-D), time-duration ratio comparisons between pattern segments, and relative symmetry validation. These capabilities are not available in the provided indicator set, which offers only price, volume, and technical indicators but no pattern detection or temporal geometry analysis functions.
translation_notes: The signal describes a sophisticated pattern validation rule based on Aronson's principle of geometric proportionality in head-and-shoulders formations. The core requirement—validating that penetration time does not exceed 1.0x the A-to-E formation duration—cannot be expressed using only the allowed indicators (close, sma, rsi, atr, bb, macd, adx, stoch, dc, pivots, volume, returns). A proper implementation would require a dedicated pattern recognition module that can: (1) identify 5-point pattern structures, (2) measure absolute time intervals between specific pattern points, (3) compute time ratios relative to pattern geometry, and (4) validate relative symmetry constraints. Partial proxy conditions have been suggested using neckline penetration (pivot cross) and volume confirmation, but these do not capture the essential time-ratio validation logic.
